2603.09374.title: MIL-PF: Multiple Instance Learning on Precomputed Features for Mammography Classification
2603.09374.paper_summary: Modern foundation models provide highly expressive visual representations, yet adapting them to high-resolution medical imaging remains challenging due to limited annotations and weak supervision. Mammography, in particular, is characterized by large images, variable multi-view studies and predominantly breast-level labels, making end-to-end fine-tuning computationally expensive and often impractical. We propose Multiple Instance Learning on Precomputed Features (MIL-PF), a scalable framework that combines frozen foundation encoders with a lightweight MIL head for mammography classification. By precomputing the semantic representations and training only a small task-specific aggregation module (40k parameters), the method enables efficient experimentation and adaptation without retraining large backbones. The architecture explicitly models the global tissue context and the sparse local lesion signals through attention-based aggregation. MIL-PF achieves state-of-the-art classification performance at clinical scale while substantially reducing training complexity. We release the code for full reproducibility.
2603.09374.paper_summary_zh: 現代基礎模型提供了高度表現力的視覺表示，但由於註釋有限和監督不足，將其應用於高解析度醫學影像仍然具有挑戰性。乳房攝影尤其以大型影像、多視角研究和主要以乳房為單位的標籤為特徵，使得端到端的微調在計算上昂貴且往往不切實際。我們提出了基於預計算特徵的多實例學習（MIL-PF），這是一個可擴展的框架，將凍結的基礎編碼器與輕量級的MIL頭結合，用於乳房攝影分類。通過預計算語義表示並僅訓練一個小型任務特定聚合模組（40k參數），該方法能夠在不重新訓練大型主幹的情況下進行高效的實驗和適應。該架構通過基於注意力的聚合明確建模了全局組織上下文和稀疏的局部病變信號。MIL-PF在臨床規模上實現了最先進的分類性能，同時大幅降低了訓練複雜性。我們釋放了代碼以實現完全的可重現性。
2603.09374.author: Nikola Jovišić et.al.
2603.09374.authors: Nikola Jovišić, Milica Škipina, Nicola Dall'Asen, Dubravko Ćulibrk
